auth_notes: Comdata primarily uses SOAP-based web services for fleet and virtual card management. Authentication typically requires a combination of Account Code, Customer ID, and WS-Security headers (UsernameToken). Some newer REST endpoints use API keys, but IP whitelisting is almost always mandatory.
rate_limits: Contract-specific; typically restricted to 5-10 requests per second for standard implementations.
gotchas: Heavy reliance on XML/SOAP requires significant manual data mapping and use of the n8n XML node or custom JavaScript.
Strict IP whitelisting is standard, which can be problematic for n8n Cloud users without a static outbound proxy.
Production access requires a rigorous certification process with a Comdata implementation manager.
client_must_provide: Static IP address of the n8n instance for whitelisting
WSDL files for specific service endpoints
research_notes: The integration will require the HTTP Request node to send POST requests with XML payloads. Developers must manually construct the SOAP Envelope. Because Comdata's responses are in XML, a 'XML to JSON' node will be necessary after every request. If using n8n Cloud, a static IP service like QuotaGuard may be required to pass Comdata's firewall.
